# Trigger phrases — what to say to invoke each front-* skill

**Generated by `scripts/build_triggers.py`** from every shipped skill's `SKILL.md` description. **Do not hand-edit** — a test in `tests/test_triggers_md.py` asserts this file equals the generated output on every commit. To change a phrase, edit the relevant `SKILL.md` and re-run the generator.

The trigger phrases below are what each skill's frontmatter description claims will activate it in Claude Code / OpenCode. They are not the only phrases that work — the agent matches prompts to the description holistically — but they are the ones the maintainer guarantees.

| Trigger phrase | Activates | Status |
|---|---|---|

## How discovery works

Claude Code / OpenCode read every installed skill's `SKILL.md` frontmatter at conversation start. When the user's prompt matches a description, that skill is loaded and its instructions become available. The agent matches semantically — phrases above are illustrative, not exhaustive. Say what you mean; the right skill should fire.

## Adding a new trigger

1. Edit the relevant `<skill>/SKILL.md` `description` field; add the new quoted phrase to the existing Trigger-phrase list.
2. Re-run `python scripts/build_triggers.py`.
3. Commit both files together — the test will refuse a commit where they drift.
